steps.title: Find a concrete sound word
steps.content: The student wants a specific example of onomatopoeia, not a definition or a general statement about language patterns. The notes say that onomatopoeias are words that imitate the sounds they represent, and they give actual heartbeat examples from Mandarin Chinese and Malay. The best choice must use one of those concrete sound words.

steps.title: Reject general statements
steps.content:
• Choice A - 'The languages of Mandarin Chinese and Malay use different words to represent the sound of a heartbeat.': This compares two languages but does not give a specific onomatopoeic word.
  • Choice B - 'Differences in how languages are structured can cause variations in the words used in different languages to represent the same sound.': This explains why variation can happen, but it remains general.
  • Choice C - 'The term "onomatopoeia," which refers to words that imitate the sound they represent, combines the Greek words for "name" and "to make."': This defines the term and gives its origin, not an example.
The remaining choice names the Malay word "dup dap dup dap" and says it both imitates and represents the sound of a heartbeat. The answer is D.
